records.ety: [Cf. OF. embruer, also embruver, embreuver, embrever, to give to drink, soak (see pref. En-, 1, 1st In-, and Breverage), but also OE. enbrewen, enbrowen, to stain, soil (cf. Brewis).]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imbureed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Imbureing.]

defs: To wet or moisten; to soak; to drench, especially in blood.
quotes: While Darwen stream, will blood of Scots imbrued.
